statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 13 of 15 countries holding critical diesel reserves and Brent crude at $111.21 amid Middle East supply disruptions.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as the majority of EU countries struggle to maintain adequate emergency reserves. While EU-wide averages show petrol at 94.5 days and diesel at 95.8 days—marginally above the 90-day mandatory minimum—this masks severe disparities across member states. Romania presents the most critical situation with diesel reserves at just 20.2 days, followed by Poland at 71 days and Austria at 74.8 days. Thirteen of fifteen countries analyzed hold diesel reserves at critical levels below safe thresholds.

Jet fuel reserves present an even more concerning picture at 89.9 days EU-wide average, falling below the mandatory minimum. Poland's jet fuel reserves are particularly alarming at 21.3 days, with Portugal at 23.1 days. Only Belgium maintains adequate jet fuel stocks at 97.6 days. The aviation fuel shortage could significantly impact European air transport and connectivity.

Oil prices continue climbing with Brent crude reaching $111.21 per barrel, up 1.31% and approximately 27% since the current Middle East conflict began. This price pressure, combined with depleted strategic reserves due to recent releases during supply disruptions, leaves the EU in a vulnerable position. Consumer fuel prices reflect this strain, with petrol averaging €1.789 per liter and diesel at €2.00 per liter, though significant variation exists between countries like Malta (€1.34) and Netherlands (€2.334) for petrol.
keyPoints: 13 out of 15 EU countries hold critical diesel reserves, with Romania at dangerously low 20.2 days
EU jet fuel reserves at 89.9 days fall below the 90-day mandatory minimum for emergency stocks
Brent crude oil has surged 27% since Middle East conflict began, now at $111.21 per barrel
Eastern European countries show the most severe shortfalls, with Poland and Romania facing critical shortages across all fuel types
Strategic reserve releases during recent supply disruptions have left the EU with limited buffer capacity for future shocks
